name: Transnevada Route H
name_original: Pico Veleta desde Capileira — paseo en BTT por el Parque Nacional de Sierra Nevada
description: This difficult 26.2-mile mountain biking route through Sierra Nevada National Park offers stunning views and challenging terrain.
region_intro: Hiking around Bubión offers access to the dramatic landscapes of the Poqueira Gorge and the Sierra Nevada National Park. The region is characterized by steep ravines, ancient woodlands, and open mountain terrain, with the village itself situated at an altitude of 1,300 meters (4,430 ft). Hikers can experience varied topography, from winding river valleys to panoramic viewpoints overlooking Spain's highest peaks and, on clear days, the Mediterranean Sea. Best hiking trails around Bubión The most popular hiking route is Bubión Village – Capileira Village loop from Bubión, a 7.8 miles (12.5 km) moderate trail that takes about 4 hours 17 minutes to complete, connecting two of the picturesque white villages in the Poqueira Gorge. Another top favourite among local hikers is Capileira Village – Bubión Village loop from Bubión, an easy 3.0 miles (4.8 km) path. This route offers scenic views between the villages and can be completed in about 1 hour 35 minutes. Local hikers also love the Loma de Cascajar Negro – Puerto Molina Viewpoint loop from Loma de las Tonadas, a 9.9 miles (15.9 km) difficult trail leading through high mountain terrain with significant elevation gain, often completed in about 5 hours 3 minutes. Hiking around Bubión is defined by its dramatic Poqueira Gorge, access to the Sierra Nevada's towering peaks, and the charm of its white villages. The network offers options for easy walks through villages, moderate trails connecting them, and challenging ascents into the higher mountains. The routes in Bubión are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 300 reviews. More than 1600 hikers have used komoot to explore Bubión's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of hiking trails can I expect around Bubión?
A: Bubión, nestled in the Poqueira Gorge and part of the Sierra Nevada National Park, offers a diverse range of trails. You'll find everything from easy walks connecting the picturesque white villages of Bubión, Pampaneira, and Capileira, to moderate routes through ancient woodlands and pine forests, and challenging ascents into the high mountain terrain of the Sierra Nevada.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly hiking options in Bubión?
A: Yes, there are several easy trails suitable for families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10304933" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Capileira Village – Bubión Village loop from Bubión&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.0-mile (4.8 km) path that offers scenic views between the villages and can be completed in about 1 hour 35 minutes. Overall, there are 7 easy routes available in the area.

Q: What are some challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, routes leading into the higher Sierra Nevada mountains are available. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14601254" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loma de Cascajar Negro – Puerto Molina Viewpoint loop from Loma de las Tonadas&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 9.9-mile (15.9 km) trail with significant elevation gain, typically taking around 5 hours 3 minutes. The region offers 6 difficult routes for those looking for strenuous climbs.

Q: Are there any circular walks available from Bubión?
A: Yes, many routes around Bubión are designed as circular walks, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6881978" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bubión Village – Capileira Village loop from Bubión&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 7.8-mile (12.5 km) trail connecting two of the white villages in the Poqueira Gorge.

Q: What natural attractions or landmarks can I see along the trails?
A: The trails around Bubión offer stunning natural beauty. You can discover the dramatic Poqueira Gorge itself, with its lush countryside and the winding Poqueira River. Highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5288705" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tajo de Cortés Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, and various viewpoints offering panoramic vistas of the Sierra Nevada peaks and, on clear days, the Mediterranean Sea. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/181587" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hondera Lagoon&lt;/a&gt; is another notable natural feature.

Q: Is it possible to hike to high peaks like Mulhacén from Bubión?
A: Bubión serves as a gateway to the Sierra Nevada National Park, making ascents to high peaks like Mulhacén and Pico del Veleta accessible from the region. These routes are generally for experienced mountaineers and may require very good fitness and alpine experience. Permits might be required for certain areas or during specific seasons, so it's advisable to check with the Sierra Nevada National Park authorities beforehand.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Bubión?
A: The best time to hike in Bubión largely depends on your preferred experience. Spring (April-May) is ideal for wildflowers and pleasant temperatures. Autumn (September-October) offers cooler weather and beautiful fall colors. Summer can be hot, especially at lower altitudes, but higher mountain trails provide cooler air. Winter hiking is possible, but higher elevations may require snow gear and experience.

Q: Are there any facilities or places to rest along the hiking routes?
A: Yes, the white villages of Bubión, Pampaneira, and Capileira offer cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options. Along some longer routes, you might find mountain huts or bivouac shelters like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/670167" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carihuela Bivouac Shelter&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4473243" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Francisco Hostel&lt;/a&gt;, which can provide a place to rest or stay overnight.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Bubión?
A: The trails in Bubión are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 300 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the stunning panoramic views, the unique charm of the white villages, the varied terrain from gorges to high mountains, and the well-maintained network of paths that cater to all skill levels.

Q: Are there any trails that feature waterfalls or significant water elements?
A: Yes, the Poqueira Gorge is carved by the Poqueira River, and the area features various streams and ancient water channels. You can find waterfalls such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5288705" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tajo de Cortés Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, adding to the natural beauty and providing serene soundscapes during your hike.

Q: Is parking available near the trailheads in Bubión?
A: Yes, parking is generally available in and around Bubión, often near the village entrances or designated areas. It's advisable to arrive early during peak season to secure a spot, especially if starting a popular route directly from the village.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete Transnevada Route H?
faqs.answer: This mountain biking route is approximately 42 kilometers long and generally takes around 4 to 5 hours to complete. However, the actual duration can vary based on your fitness level, riding pace, and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The route features a varied and challenging terrain, primarily running at elevations between 1,500 and 2,000 meters. You'll encounter a mix of dirt trails, gravel tracks, and some paved sections. Expect challenging chunky rock descents, narrow paths, steep slopes, and even boulder fields. Be prepared for occasional muddy sections and, depending on the season, even snow. There are also short sections that require you to dismount and walk your bike (hike-a-bike) due to obstacles or very steep terrain.

faqs.question: Is Transnevada Route H suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is rated as difficult and is not suitable for beginners. It demands strong endurance, advanced mountain biking skills, and prior experience with challenging alpine terrain. Riders should be prepared for significant ascents and technical descents, including hike-a-bike sections.

faqs.question: What are the best times of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are considered the ideal seasons for riding this route. During these times, you'll experience more stable weather, moderate temperatures, and fewer crowds, making for a more enjoyable and safer experience.

faqs.question: Are there any notable points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route offers several interesting sights. You'll pass through beautiful Pine Forests on the Veleta Trail, the Puerto Molina bivouac, and the scenic Paso de los Franceses. You'll also get views of the stunning Laguna de la Caldera, offering breathtaking alpine landscapes.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Transnevada Route H shares sections with several other trails. Notably, it covers 44% of the official Transnevada Ruta H, 24% of the Integral de Sierra Nevada, and 16% of the Subida al Mulhacén. You'll also find it intersecting with parts of the Carretera Antigua GR-411 de Capileira al Veleta.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the trailhead for Transnevada Route H?
faqs.answer: While the specific starting point for this segment isn't detailed, the broader Transnevada Route is a circular trail around the Sierra Nevada National Park with numerous signposted access points. For this particular route, which starts near Capileira, you would typically look for parking in or around the village to begin your ride.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Transnevada Route H?
faqs.answer: The Sierra Nevada National Park generally allows dogs, but they must be kept on a leash at all times to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Always check local signage for any specific restrictions in certain areas.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this trail?
faqs.answer: As the Transnevada Route H is located within the Sierra Nevada National Park, there are generally no entrance fees to access the park itself. However, for certain activities or specific protected areas, permits might be required. It's always advisable to check the official Sierra Nevada National Park website for the most current regulations regarding permits, especially if you plan to camp or deviate from marked trails.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife and plants can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The Sierra Nevada National Park is a biodiversity hotspot, home to over 2,100 plant species, many of which are endemic to the region. You'll experience dramatic alpine landscapes and wildflower-covered peaks. While specific wildlife sightings are never guaranteed, the park is known for its ibex, eagles, and various bird species. Keep an eye out for the unique flora and fauna that thrive in this high-altitude environment.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this route?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and high-altitude environment, you should bring appropriate mountain biking gear, including a well-maintained bike, helmet, and protective padding. Essential items include plenty of water, high-energy snacks, navigation tools (like a GPS device or komoot app), a first-aid kit, and repair tools for your bike. Be prepared for sudden temperature changes by carrying layers of clothing, including waterproof and windproof outerwear.

waypoints.name: Pine Forests on the Veleta Trail
